Tests object behavior with overridden `valueOf` and `toString` methods in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which override the default behavior of these methods in JavaScript. The `valueOf` method returns the number `1`, and the `toString` method returns the string representation of `0`. The code then assigns this object to a new variable `copyObject`, creating a reference to the same object. An assertion checks that when `2` is added to `object`, the result is equal to adding `2` to `copyObject`, and that `object` itself is equal to `copyObject + 2`. This demonstrates how JavaScript handles object references and type coercion during arithmetic operations.
